name: ETH Persistence Squeeze Release v1
edge_mechanism: compression release confirmed by post-break persistence rather than first breakout bar
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner similar-asset lane for a persistence-based squeeze release. The edge should come from entering after release bars prove temporary acceptance above fast value, not from blindly paying for the first expansion print.
invalidation_condition: Abort if compression releases remain too sparse, if persistence is not durable after the hold, or if management cannot offset false-release churn.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Compression resolves into expansion, then price holds above fast value long enough to earn a follow-through continuation entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: When market structure transitions toward trend-up, not every breakout persists, but those that do often offer a second-leg entry after the first release proves acceptance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is a clean lane because compression-release structure is visible without the chaos of thinner assets.

indicator_role_map.EMA_55: deeper trend context and back-fail exit reference

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If releases resolve into one-bar exhaustion instead of persistent acceptance, the continuation thesis dies immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If persistence appears but does not travel far enough, compress exits before changing the release detector.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Selective trades with decent first-target capture and limited but real runner contribution on stronger releases.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h plus one cross-lane produce tradable density and positive post-cost continuation after release persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, too few setups, or post-release holds consistently fail to continue.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; squeeze and persistence are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by moderate compression thresholds rather than extreme rarity

test_lanes.why_included: Cleanest lane for persistence-based release continuation.
test_lanes.continue_if: Release-hold setups show credible density and positive follow-through after costs.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Setups are too sparse or persistence fails to extend.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: A modest number of clean post-release continuation entries.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same release-persistence mechanism survives larger volatility swings.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO preserves the mechanism with acceptable noise.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO turns the setup into pure volatility noise.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Fewer but larger release-persistence events than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the persistence filter is robust enough for a more fragile high-beta lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS still prints a recognizable second-leg release behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: False-release churn overwhelms the hold logic.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Patchier participation but occasional large second-leg moves.

long: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
short: Exit remaining 20% on close back through EMA_55 or 4 bars
